## [03:11 - 06:16]

Oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh I thought you were speaking a little bit quickly And that's the only thing they'll say even if they notice three or four other things that you can do to improve They're not going to tell you now. Here's the truth that I wanted to get to There's two parts to it the first part is that most people don't know how to give feedback in a way that's going to be well received and Chances are you don't know how to receive feedback. I Can give you an example right now Let's say that someone was giving me some feedback and sharing their thoughts with me when I first started to receive feedback This is what happened. I'd listen to them and they'd say to me you touch your glasses a lot You do it like 50 times in an hour and it's really distracting and then the second thing you do is some of the magic tricks It doesn't really connect with the point you're talking about and then that also becomes No, please keep keep keep sharing. This is good. I want this. Okay. Just doesn't really seem like you want to listen to me I don't know why why do you find pretty much? The reason why we do this is simply because the majority of the time when we're asking for feedback What we're actually asking for is praise and one of the main reasons for that is because your voice is your personality Your voice is your personality This is a piece of wisdom that one of my vocal coaches taught me and when you receive feedback on your personality It feels like a personal attack That's why you have to go to a workshop spend time with an expert be in the right space right environment and right frame of mind Don't go asking randoms or not experts in this particular field You're not going to ask a random about nutrition advice or medical advice You'd go talk to a doctor and you talk to a nutritionist Make sure you go and ask the right person ask an expert now If you're going to ask a random anything and you're going to ask people anything after a presentation If you have enough self-awareness to go I didn't think that presentation was very good Instead of saying hey, do you have any feedback for me? What you can ask is the following. Hey look I Feel like communication is an area. I should probably improve Do you think it's something I should do if you want Feedback for the purpose of improving your communication skills and you're not looking for praise Then the best place to get this is to get it from an expert No one wants to be mean no one wants to tell you the truth so instead of putting all that pressure on people look for a Communication expert communication teacher communication coach a tender workshop Because then you're putting yourself in the right space the right frame of mind to receive that feedback

## [06:16 - 06:59]

Outside of that environment. It's very difficult to accept and receive feedback and that is how you actually Can improve your communication skills and that's it. That's the truth.
